# Transcrição: 11-How to Create Separate Ad Account

- **Fonte:** 11-How to Create Separate Ad Account
- **Duração:** 04:44
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay guys, so this is kind of another important one in terms of prepping the account for a client and actually this is a funny one because you shouldn't just have one account so we should say account, plural. Now as those of you that have run ads in the past or have been working with Facebook for a while you'll be well aware that Facebook goes through phases where it will call ad accounts. So we just went through one of those recently where a lot of ad accounts just got cut off and sometimes it's purely because of negative feedback on the account relative to positive feedback. Other times it's just it the bot just goes through and weeds out you know industries that it's not a really big fan of like supplements etc etc. So what we need to do whenever we're working with a client it's just good practice to get into is part of the SOP is to set up a backup ad account. Now this is done through business manager however it's really really important to state here that if you create the ad account you will always own that ad account right if you create the ad account in your business manager you'll always own it. So you should really be getting the client to create this unless you are leasing you're on a least funnel pricing model with them if they're paying you upfront or a monthly fee where they own all the assets created etc. They really need to create that ad account and then you need to request access with the same way you would request access to any other account. So you simply explain this to the client you explain the strategy on why we're doing this and you get them to create a separate ad account and then you request access through your business manager. Now here's what you're going to do you're going to create basically two ad accounts one of those ad accounts you're not going to run any campaigns out of you're simply using the pixel and you're going to share that pixel to the other ad account using business managers. So when you're inside of business manager let me just show you here under business settings you'll see you've got a pixel tab here and you can actually give access so you can assign ad accounts to different pixels for example. Okay so what you want to do is you want to actually share that pixel into the other account where you will be running all of your campaigns from. So you're not going to be using the pixel from that second ad account you're going to be using it from the first the pixel from the first ad account but you're going to run all your campaigns out of the second ad account using the pixel from the first ad account I hope that makes sense. The reason for that is more often than not when an ad account gets shut down you can still use

## [03:00 - 04:44]

the pixel however there are cases and their instances where you cannot use the pixel so what you don't want to get into is a situation where an ad account gets shut down and then you lose all your data point blank so all that advertising spend you've put on all that data you've collected is just gone. So just to mitigate your risk and the risks to the client it's just much easier to use a clean pixel from an ad account that isn't really running any campaign so there's no risk that it would get shut down. What that means is that if you ever hit a glitch where an ad account gets shut down all you have to do is right away while you're disputing it with Facebook just create another ad account so get your client to create another ad account share the pixel from the first ad account and you're pretty much ready to go right away you can even try to share audiences from the ad account that was shut down etc but it means that you're not sitting there on your hands for three weeks waiting for Facebook to turn an ad account back on you've got your pixel you've got all the data that's what's actually the most important part right of your advertising is the health of that pixel so this is just a good practice to get into and something that we would do for all the clients that we work with is to just run two ad accounts and one of them is purely for a pixel in case we ever hit hiccups regardless of the industry that you're in there's always extenuating circumstances where even the most innocent ad account or product will get shut down so it's just a good practice to get into and it's part of the SOP as well guys
